req.title: Inter-facility referral (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-092 mandates three verifiable slices: (1) happy-path referral creation by an authenticated clinician that stores the referral, dispatches a secure message, checks consent, and emits an AuditEvent; (2) rejection with HTTP 403 when patient consent is absent; (3) a boundary case exercising the minimum-viable referral payload (exactly 1 character subject note) to probe edge-of-valid input. Each test is isolated via fixture-supplied auth tokens and ephemeral patient/facility IDs so they can run in any order without shared state. AuditEvent verification is performed by querying the FHIR AuditEvent endpoint filtered by the referral entity reference, satisfying the end-to-end audit mandate. Heuristics selected: EP for the nominal flow, NEG for the consent-absent 403, and BVA for the minimal-payload boundary.

tests.name: test_referral_happy_path_stores_resource_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Target facility ID available via <target_facility_id> fixture
Patient has active consent recorded in the system
FHIR-Lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with valid clinician token, consented patient ID, and target facility ID
Assert HTTP 201 and capture referral ID from response Location header
Assert at least one AuditEvent record is returned
expected: Response body contains resourceType=ReferralRequest and a server-assigned id
AuditEvent query returns HTTP 200 with total >= 1
trace: R-HLT-092: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_referral_happy_path_stores_resource_and_emits_audit(
    base_url, clinician_auth_token, consented_patient_id, target_facility_id
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{consented_patient_id}"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": f"Organization/{target_facility_id}"}],
        "note": [{"text": "Routine cardiology referral"}]
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest", json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, post_resp.text
    referral_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
    assert referral_id, "Server must assign an id"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"ReferralRequest/{referral_id}"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = audit_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, "At least one AuditEvent must exist"
    actions = [e["resource"]["action"]
               for e in bundle.get("entry", [])]
    assert "C" in actions, "AuditEvent action 'C' (create) must be present"

tests.name: test_referral_rejected_403_when_consent_absent
preconditions: Patient has NO active consent recorded (non_consented_patient_id fixture)
Target facility ID available via <target_facility_id> fixture
FHIR-Lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with valid clinician token but non-consented patient ID
Assert no ReferralRequest resource was persisted
GET /AuditEvent for the attempted referral and confirm a denial AuditEvent exists
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.code=forbidden
No ReferralRequest resource created for this patient
AuditEvent with outcome != 0 (failure) is recorded for the denied attempt
trace: R-HLT-092: error pathway - 403 when consent absent
R-HLT-092: AuditEvent emitted for every operation (including denied ones)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_referral_rejected_403_when_consent_absent(
    base_url, clinician_auth_token, non_consented_patient_id, target_facility_id
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{non_consented_patient_id}"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": f"Organization/{target_facility_id}"}],
        "note": [{"text": "Neurology consult"}]
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest", json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403, got {post_resp.status_code}: {post_resp.text}"
    )
    body = post_resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    codes = [i.get("code") for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert "forbidden" in codes, "OperationOutcome must cite 'forbidden'"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"patient": non_consented_patient_id, "action": "C",
                "outcome": "8"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert audit_resp.json().get("total", 0) >= 1, (
        "A failed AuditEvent (outcome!=0) must be recorded even for denied ops"
    )

tests.name: test_referral_boundary_minimal_note_single_character
preconditions: Patient has active consent recorded (consented_patient_id fixture)
Target facility ID available via <target_facility_id> fixture
FHIR-Lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with a note.text value of exactly 1 character ('A') — the minimum non-empty string
GET the created ReferralRequest by ID and verify note.text is preserved as 'A'
Confirm AuditEvent is emitted for the creation
expected: HTTP 201 Created on POST /ReferralRequest with 1-char note
AuditEvent with action 'C' exists for the created referral ID
No validation error is raised for a single-character note
trace: R-HLT-092: referral stored (boundary input: minimal note length = 1 char)
R-HLT-092: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_referral_boundary_minimal_note_single_character(
    base_url, clinician_auth_token, consented_patient_id, target_facility_id
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    minimal_note = "A"  # BVA: lower boundary of non-empty string
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{consented_patient_id}"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": f"Organization/{target_facility_id}"}],
        "note": [{"text": minimal_note}]
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest", json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201, post_resp.text
    referral_id = post_resp.json()["id"]

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    stored = get_resp.json()
    assert stored["note"][0]["text"] == minimal_note, (
        "Server must preserve exact 1-char note text"
    )

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"ReferralRequest/{referral_id}"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert audit_resp.json().get("total", 0) >= 1, (
        "AuditEvent must be emitted even for boundary-input referrals"
    )
